title: Support assignment patterns as comparison operands
body:
## Summary

This patch adds support for assignment patterns (`'{...}`) as operands of
comparison operators (EQ, NEQ, etc.). Previously these were rejected with
`UNSUPPORTED: Assignment pattern member not underneath a supported construct`.
IEEE 1800-2023 sections 7.8, 10.9.1, and 7.11 allow assignment patterns in
equality comparisons with arrays and structs.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t;
  int arr[4];
  initial begin
    arr = '{10, 20, 30, 40};
    if (arr == '{10, 20, 30, 40}) $display("match");
  end
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t.v:5:17: Unsupported/Illegal: Assignment pattern member
    not underneath a supported construct: EQ
```

## Changes

- In `visit_cmp_eq_gt` (`V3Width.cpp`), detect when one operand is an
  `AstPattern` and iterate the non-pattern operand first, then propagate
  its resolved dtype to the pattern side via `WidthVP` -- analogous to
  how assignments pass LHS dtype to RHS patterns
- Updated `t_stream_unpack_lhs.out` golden file -- the pattern UNSUPPORTED
  errors in that test are now resolved, unmasking a pre-existing V3Const
  internal error on stream unpacking width

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_assign_pattern_cmp.{v,py}` -- 12 scenarios:
  fixed-size array, queue slice, packed struct, nested struct array,
  pattern on LHS/RHS, EQ/NEQ, different widths, negative match
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_stream_unpack_lhs.out` -- golden file
  reflects changed error output

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
